story_title: Ukraine-Russia war: drone escalation, corruption probes, and peace signals
tldr: Ukraine war headlines fracture into three distinct advertised stories: frontline drone escalation (SECURITY_DEFENSE) dominates real-time coverage; corruption and sanctions headlines (CRIME_PUNISHMENT) compete for space in Western outlets; and peace-talk positioning (POLITICAL) runs as a third track in European and Turkish coverage. Religious press uniquely foregrounds civilian grief.

evidence.quote: incoming Russian drone attacks during daylight
Russian Regions Move Schools Online Amid Drone Attack Fears
massive daytime attack with over 200 drones across Ukraine
Belarus's Lukashenko: 'We Are All Preparing for War'

frames.sub_frame: corruption probe and sanctions against Russia

evidence.quote: named as suspect in major money-laundering probe
ex-chief of staff appears in court in money-laundering case
Donbas-tied arms dealers peddling guns for Kim Jong Un
EU Takes Aim at Russia's Abductions of Ukrainian Children
Putin's assertion that Ukraine war is nearly over
Putin floats ex-German chancellor as Ukraine mediator
no clear roadmap to end Ukraine war despite Putin's words
EU will decide its negotiator for Ukraine peace talks
'My son was taken away by war. Now I am everyone's mother'
1 800 monuments du patrimoine culturel ukrainien endommagés

bottom_line: Ukraine headline framing is unusually fragmented: security outlets lead with the drone escalation, Western liberal outlets lead with corruption probes and sanctions, and European and Turkish political desks lead with peace-signal positioning — all on the same day, advertising three different stories about the same conflict.
